hero.heading: 🔨 Jenkins - Automate Your Entire DevOps Pipeline
hero.subtitle: Build, test, and deploy applications automatically on every code change with zero manual intervention.
hero.whyLearn: Most used CI/CD tool in the world. Powers millions of deployments daily. If you do DevOps, you need Jenkins.

storytelling.problem: Every code change needs manual building, testing, and deploying. Mistakes happen. Takes hours. Your team wastes time on repetitive tasks.
storytelling.solution: Jenkins automates everything. Code pushed → auto-builds → auto-tests → auto-deploys. Errors caught instantly. Fast feedback loop.
storytelling.analogy: Jenkins is a factory assembly line. Code goes in one end, tested product comes out the other, automatically. Quality gates prevent bad products from shipping.
storytelling.realWorldExample: Netflix uses Jenkins to deploy thousands of times per day with zero downtime. Every code change triggers automated testing and deployment.
storytelling.deepDive: Jenkins uses plugins for endless integration possibilities with Git, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Slack. Master-agent architecture scales to thousands of jobs running in parallel.

architectureFlow.title: Define Pipeline
architectureFlow.description: Write Jenkinsfile describing build, test, deploy stages
architectureFlow.details: Declarative or scripted pipeline defines your entire process. Version controlled alongside code.

architectureFlow.title: Trigger on Event
architectureFlow.description: Jenkins watches Git repo. On push, automatically runs pipeline.
architectureFlow.details: Webhooks notify Jenkins instantly when code changes. Branch-specific pipelines for dev/staging/prod.

architectureFlow.title: Build & Test
architectureFlow.description: Compile code, run unit tests, integration tests, security scans
architectureFlow.details: Fail fast if tests fail. Don't proceed to deploy. Parallel test execution speeds up feedback.

architectureFlow.title: Deploy
architectureFlow.description: If all tests pass, automatically deploy to production
architectureFlow.details: Zero downtime deployments with blue-green or canary strategies. Auto-rollback on failures.

beginner.description: Define build/test/deploy stages as code in Jenkinsfile
Extend Jenkins with Git, Docker, Kubernetes integration
Store passwords, tokens securely for production deployments
Auto-trigger pipelines on Git push events

intermediate.description: Write complex logic in Jenkinsfile for conditional deployments
Reuse pipeline code across jobs to reduce duplication
Modern UI for visualizing pipelines and logs
Alert teams on build success/failure via Slack, email
Store build outputs, test reports for later analysis

advanced.description: Run builds in K8s pods for dynamic scaling
Deploy using declarative configuration from Git
Integrate SAST, DAST, dependency checks into pipeline
Auto-create jobs for Git branches with different deployments
Cache dependencies, parallelize builds to speed up feedback

commands.whatItDoes: Starts a build of the specified job immediately
commands.realUseCase: Trigger deployments from scripts or other tools. Automate cross-system workflows.
commands.commonMistake: Not specifying the correct Jenkins URL results in connection refused errors

commands.cmd: pipeline { agent any; stages { stage('Build') { steps { sh 'npm run build' } } } }
commands.whatItDoes: Creates a multi-stage pipeline with defined jobs and steps
commands.realUseCase: Industry standard way to define CI/CD workflows. Version controlled in Jenkinsfile.
commands.example: Jenkinsfile in git repo automatically triggers on commit
commands.commonMistake: Mixing declarative and scripted syntax causes syntax errors

commands.description: Collect test results after build completes
commands.whatItDoes: Parses JUnit XML and displays test results in UI
commands.realUseCase: Visualize test results, track trends, fail builds if tests fail
commands.example: Jenkins shows pass/fail rate, allows drilling into failed tests
commands.commonMistake: Not collecting results means test failures are missed

commands.description: Use stored credentials securely in pipeline
commands.whatItDoes: Injects credentials as environment variables without exposing in logs
commands.realUseCase: Push Docker images to registry, deploy to cloud, authenticate to databases
commands.example: Jenkins masks credential values in build logs automatically
commands.commonMistake: Hardcoding credentials in Jenkinsfile exposes secrets to everyone

comparisons.title: Jenkins vs GitLab CI vs GitHub Actions

left.name: 🔨 Jenkins
points: Most powerful and flexible CI/CD tool

comparisons.whenToUse: Use Jenkins for large enterprises with complex requirements. Use GitHub Actions for small teams. Use GitLab CI if you use GitLab.

beginnerMistakes.title: Hardcoding Secrets in Jenkinsfile
beginnerMistakes.description: Passwords visible in version control and build logs. Everyone can see them.
beginnerMistakes.howToFix: Use Jenkins Credentials Store. Inject via withCredentials(). Never commit secrets.

beginnerMistakes.title: Not Setting Build Timeouts
beginnerMistakes.description: Hanging builds consume resources and block other jobs. Pipelines run forever if stuck.
beginnerMistakes.howToFix: Set timeout(time: 30, unit: 'MINUTES') in pipeline. Kill hung builds automatically.

realWorldUsage.name: Netflix
realWorldUsage.usage: Netflix uses Jenkins to deploy code thousands of times per day with automated testing.

realWorldUsage.name: Google
realWorldUsage.usage: Google uses Jenkins for CI/CD pipelines across internal tools and infrastructure.

realWorldUsage.name: LinkedIn
realWorldUsage.usage: LinkedIn relies on Jenkins for continuous deployment of microservices.

productionSection.scaling: Use Jenkins agents to distribute builds. Kubernetes plugin scales agents dynamically. Parallelized stages reduce pipeline time.
productionSection.security: Use Credentials Store for secrets. RBAC to control access. Regular backups of Jenkins configuration. Scan dependencies for vulnerabilities.
productionSection.monitoring: Monitor Jenkins server health (disk, memory, CPU). Track build trends and failures. Alert on long-running builds.
productionSection.cicd: Use shared libraries for reusable code. Version control Jenkinsfiles. Implement code review gates. Automated testing before merging.

labs.title: Create Your First Pipeline
labs.description: Build a simple pipeline that compiles code and runs tests

steps.title: Create Jenkinsfile
steps.instruction: Write Jenkinsfile with Build and Test stages

steps.title: Create Jenkins Job
steps.instruction: Point to Git repo with Jenkinsfile

steps.title: Trigger Build
steps.instruction: Push code and watch pipeline run

labs.expectedOutput: Pipeline completes successfully with test results visible

interviewQuestions.question: What is the difference between declarative and scripted pipelines?
interviewQuestions.answer: Declarative uses simpler syntax for common use cases. Scripted uses Groovy for complex logic. Declarative is easier; scripted is more powerful.
interviewQuestions.example: Use declarative for standard build/test/deploy. Use scripted for conditional logic.
interviewQuestions.followUp: When would you choose scripted over declarative?
